LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS THE CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, THE SAN FRANCISCO FIRE DEPARTMENT, THE SAN FRANCISCO FIRE COMMISSION, THE CIVIL SERVICE COMMISSION OF SAN FRANCISCO, DAVID JOHNSON, CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION FOR TEMPORARY STAY OF PROCEEDINGS IS GRANTED. "[T]RIAL COURTS HAVE INHERENT POWER TO STAY TRIALS AWAITING FINAL APPELLATE RESOLUTION OF RELATED ACTIONS THAT MIGHT CONCLUSIVELY DETERMINE ALL OR SOME OF THE ISSUES IN THE STAYED ACTION." (KOCH-ASH V. SUP. CT. (1986) 180 CAL.APP.3D 689, 969.) A TEMPORARY STAY OF THIS MATTER STRONGLY PROMOTES JUDICIAL ECONOMY AND EFFICIENCY, WHICH OUTWEIGHS ANY PREJUDICE TO PLAINTIFFS. ACCORDINGLY, ALL PROCEEDINGS IN THIS MATTER ARE HEREBY STAYED UNTIL THIRTY (30) DAYS AFTER THE RULING OF THE COURT OF APPEAL IN THE CASE OF DANNER, ET AL. V. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, ET. AL, SAN FRANCISCO SUPERIOR COURT CASE NO. CGC-10-501981, BECOMES FINAL. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK FELICIA GREEN; COURT REPORTER MELANIE GHENO, CSR NO. 7489; DEPT. 302. (302/EHG)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Temporary Stay Of Proceedings
Off Calendar May-14-2015 Advanced From Jul-21-15 To Jun-08-15 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Temporary Stay Of Proceedings
Off Calendar Jun-08-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Murlene J. Randle, of the Law Offices of Murlene J. Randle, Tel. 415.352.0189, for plaintiffs;
D.A. Ruth M. Bond, of the City Attorney's Office, Tel. 415.554.3976, for defendants.

Ruling: A R G U E D. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANTS THE CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, THE SAN FRANCISCO FIRE DEPARTMENT, THE SAN FRANCISCO FIRE COMMISSION, THE CIVIL SERVICE COMMISSION OF SAN FRANCISCO, DAVID JOHNSON, CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S Motion For Temporary Stay Of Proceedings is Granted. "[T]rial courts have inherent power to stay trials awaiting final appellate resolution of related actions that might conclusively determine all or some of the issues in the stayed action." (Koch-Ash v. Sup. Ct. (1986) 180 Cal.App.3d 689, 969.) A temporary stay of this matter strongly promotes judicial economy and efficiency, which outweighs any prejudice to Plaintiffs. Accordingly, all proceedings in this matter are hereby stayed until thirty (30) days after the ruling of the Court of Appeal in the case of Danner, et al. v. City and County of San Francisco, et. al, San Francisco Superior Court case no. CGC-10-501981, becomes final. Order signed in open court. Judge Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk Felicia Green; Court Reporter Melanie Gheno, CSR No. 7489; Dept. 302. (302/EHG)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Aug-04-2014 Set For Trial On Aug-03-15 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-16-2014 Overruled
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. MURLENE J. RANDLE & ATTY. WILLIAM REED OF THE LAW OFFICES OF MURLENE J. RANDLE, TEL. NO. 352-0189 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. RUTH M. BOND, DEPUTY CITY ATTORNEY, TEL. NO. 554-3976 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS THE CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, THE SAN FRANCISCO FIRE DEPARTMENT, and THE SAN FRANCISCO FIRE COMMISSION'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS Overruled. Liberally construing the complaint, Defendant David Johnson was performing operational acts in facilitating and overseeing the examination process and therefore does not enjoy immunity under Gov. Code sec. 820.2. The Complaint pleads that Johnson was implementing the decisions of the Chief of the Fire Department. (Complaint, pars. 54, 60, 64.) Gov. Code sec. 822.2 immunity does not bar the 5th cause of action because the Complaint sufficiently pleads actual fraud. (Complaint, pars. 268-283.) Defendants' argument about summary adjudication being granted in the Danner case is irrelevant. A demurrer does not consider whether Plaintiffs can actually prove their causes of action, only whether they are adequately pled. Worker's Compensation Exclusivity does not bar the negligence claims because Plaintiffs allege economic injuries that are not compensable under the Worker's Compensation scheme. (Complaint, pars. 295, 306; Singh v. Southland Stone USA (2010) 186 Cal.App.4th 338, 365-66, "only injuries to a worker's person, as opposed to his property, are compensable.") The Complaint adequately provides a statutory basis for the sixth and seventh causes of action. (C.A. v. William S. Hart Union High School Dist. (2012) 53 Cal.4th 861, 868, "the general rule is that an employee of a public entity is liable for his torts to the same extent as a private person ( 820, subd. (a)) and the public entity is vicariously liable for any injury which its employee causes ( 815.2, subd. (a)) to the same extent as a private employer ( 815, subd. (b)).") All requests for judicial notice are granted. The Court did not consider arguments raised for the first time in reply. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976 =302/EHG
